2605.09502.title: Hidden Error Awareness in Chain-of-Thought Reasoning: The Signal Is Diagnostic, Not Causal
2605.09502.paper_summary: Chain-of-thought (CoT) prompting assumes that generated reasoning reflects a model's internal computation. We show this assumption is wrong in a specific, measurable way: models internally detect their own reasoning errors but outwardly express confidence in them. A linear probe on hidden states predicts trace correctness with 0.95 AUROC -- from the very first reasoning step (0.79) -- while verbalized confidence for wrong traces is 4.55/5, nearly identical to correct ones (4.87/5). A text-surface classifier achieves only 0.59 on the same data, confirming a 0.20-point gap invisible in the generated text. This hidden error awareness holds across three model families (Qwen, Llama, Phi), 1.5B-72B parameters, and RL-trained reasoning models (DeepSeek-R1, 0.852 AUROC). The natural question is whether this signal can fix the errors it detects. It cannot. Four interventions -- activation steering, probe-guided best-of-N, self-correction, and activation patching -- all fail; patching destroys output coherence entirely. The signal is diagnostic, not causal: a readout of computation quality, not a lever to redirect it. This delineates a boundary for mechanistic interpretability: error representations during reasoning are fundamentally different from the factual knowledge representations that prior work has successfully edited.
2605.09502.paper_summary_zh: Chain-of-thought (CoT) 提示假設生成的推理反映了模型的內部計算。<lb/>我們以具體且可測量的方式顯示這一假設是錯誤的：模型內部檢測到自己的推理錯誤，但外在表達出對這些錯誤的信心。<lb/>對隱藏狀態的線性探測器以 0.95 AUROC 預測追蹤的正確性——從第一個推理步驟開始（0.79）——而對錯誤追蹤的口頭信心為 4.55/5，與正確的追蹤（4.87/5）幾乎相同。<lb/>一個文本表面分類器在相同數據上僅達到 0.59，確認了在生成文本中不可見的 0.20 點差距。<lb/>這種隱藏的錯誤意識在三個模型家族（Qwen、Llama、Phi）、1.5B-72B 參數和 RL 訓練的推理模型（DeepSeek-R1，0.852 AUROC）中保持一致。<lb/>自然的問題是這個信號是否能修正它檢測到的錯誤。<lb/>它不能。<lb/>四種干預措施——激活引導、探測引導的最佳 N、 自我修正和激活修補——均失敗；修補完全破壞了輸出的一致性。<lb/>這個信號是診斷性的，而不是因果性的：它是計算質量的讀出，而不是重新引導它的杠杆。<lb/>這劃定了機械可解釋性的邊界：推理過程中的錯誤表示與先前工作成功編輯的事實知識表示根本不同。
2605.09502.author: Aojie Yuan et.al.
2605.09502.authors: Aojie Yuan, Zhiyuan Julian Su, Haiyue Zhang, Yi Nian, Yue Zhao
